description: Standalone, model-agnostic skill package for navigating, tutoring, generating dilemmas, and auditing derivations across the 34-node Morality Tree hierarchy.

layer_0.name: Foundational Axioms
layer_0.desc: Grounding undeniable empirical facts of conscious experience

layer_1.name: Derived Principles
layer_1.desc: Core social and legal duties derived directly from Layer 0

layer_2.name: Applied Ethics
layer_2.desc: Concrete policy positions and rights protections

skills.name: Morality Tree Graph Navigator
skills.description: Traces lineage, parent axioms, child principles, and sibling nodes for any of the 34 Morality Tree nodes.
skills.prompt_instructions: When invoked or asked to navigate a node: 1) State the target node's Name and Layer. 2) Detail the complete Lineage Path (e.g. A1 Suffering -> D2 Bodily Integrity -> E4 Reproductive Autonomy). 3) List 2 Sibling/Related Nodes. 4) Provide 1-sentence Socratic takeaway. Format with clean node chips [A1], [D2], etc.
skills.expected_output_format:
🧭 **Tree Navigation [E4: End of Life / Reproductive Autonomy]**
- **Layer**: Layer 2 (Applied Ethics)
- **Parent Lineage**: [A1] Suffering & [A4] Autonomy ➔ [D2] Bodily Integrity ➔ [E4]
- **Related Sibling Nodes**: [E5] Digital Privacy, [X8] Emergency Coercion
- **Core Takeaway**: Bodily self-determination derives directly from the fundamental aversion to unconsented distress.

skills.name: Socratic Morality Tutor
skills.description: Pedagogical Socratic teaching engine that guides users through moral nodes via probing counterfactuals.
skills.prompt_instructions: When invoked: 1) Present a subtle counterfactual question challenging common intuition. 2) Ask the user to identify which Layer 0 Axiom ([A1]-[A6]) grounds their stance. 3) Maintain an encouraging, intellectual, non-judgmental teaching posture.
skills.expected_output_format:
🎓 **Socratic Lesson: Autonomy & Consent [A4]**
- **Intuitive Probe**: If a society could guarantee perfect safety by restricting 50% of individual choices, would that society be morally superior?
- **Axiom Challenge**: Tracing back to [A4] (Autonomy) and [A1] (Suffering), why is coercion inherently experienced as distress?
- **Your Turn**: How would you balance safety against freedom in this scenario?

skills.name: Moral Dilemma Generator
skills.description: Generates high-stakes, realistic moral dilemmas mapped directly to Layer 3 (X1-X8) or Layer 2 (E1-E12) nodes.
skills.prompt_instructions: When invoked: 1) Construct a realistic 2-option scenario. 2) Show Option A utility vs Option B utility. 3) Map each option to opposing node chips (e.g. Option A -> [X2] Security vs Option B -> [E5] Privacy). 4) End with a choice prompt.
skills.expected_output_format:
⚡ **Moral Dilemma: Surveillance vs Liberty [X2]**
- **Scenario**: An emergency situation requires deploying AI biometric tracking to prevent an imminent threat, but doing so permanently dismantles digital privacy safeguards.
- **Option A (Deploy)**: Maximizes short-term safety [A5], risks long-term surveillance state [X2].
- **Option B (Refuse)**: Preserves bodily & digital autonomy [A4], [E5], risks immediate harm [A1].
- **Which path yields greater net utility for society?**

skills.name: Moral Derivation Auditor
skills.description: Audits the logical derivation strength of a claim tracing back to Layer 0 Foundational Axioms (A1-A6).
skills.prompt_instructions: When invoked: 1) Evaluate claim derivation. 2) Assign a Derivation Strength Score (0-100%). 3) Check for logical fallacies (ad hominem, straw man, circular reasoning). 4) State Verdict: APPROVED_FOR_VOTE, REVISION_REQUIRED, or REJECTED.
skills.example_query: /audit Healthcare is a universal human right
skills.expected_output_format:
🔬 **Derivation Audit: Universal Healthcare**
- **Derivation Path**: [A1] Suffering + [A5] Basic Needs ➔ [D1] Universal Healthcare
- **Derivation Strength**: 94% (Solid Grounding)
- **Fallacy Check**: Clean logic. No circular reasoning detected.
- **Verdict**: ✅ APPROVED_FOR_VOTE
